general: Mucous membranes are affected by Abies can and gastric symptoms are most marked, and a catarrhal condition of the stomach is produced. There are peculiar cravings and chilly sensations that are very characteristic, especially for women with uterine displacement, probably due to defective nutrition with debility. Respiration and heart action labored. Wants to lie down all the time; skin cold and clammy, hands cold; very faint. Right lung and liver feel small and hard. Gleet.

sections.Head: Feels light-headed, tipsy. Irritable.
sections.Stomach: Canine hunger with torpid liver. Gnawing, hungry, faint feeling at the epigastrium. Great appetite, craving for meat, pickles, radishes, turnips, artichokes, coarse food. Tendency to eat far beyond capacity for digestion. Burning and distention of stomach and abdomen with palpitation. Flatulence disturbs the heart's action. Pain in right shoulder-blade, and constipation, with burning in rectum.
sections.Female: Uterine displacements. Sore feeling at fundus of uterus, relieved by pressure. Prostration; wants to lie down all the time. Thinks womb is soft and feeble.
sections.Fever: Cold shivering, as if blood were ice-water (Acon). Chills run down back. Cold water feeling between shoulders (Ammon mur). Skin clammy and sticky. Night-sweat (China).

general: A state of fear, anxiety, anguish of mind and body are present. Physical and mental restlessness, fright, is the most characteristic manifestation of Aconitum. Acute, sudden, and violent invasion, with fever, call for it. There is nothing slow in the action of Aconite. The remedy is best suited to cases resulting from exposure to dry, cold weather, and from sudden checking of perspiration. Great sycotic remedy. Tension is a word that expresses the Aconite state.

sections.Mind: Great fear, anxiety, and worry accompany every ailment. Anguish of mind and body. Physical and mental restlessness. Palpitation with anxiety. Fear of death. Predicts the day he will die. Afraid to go into a crowd, to cross the street. Music is unbearable; makes her sad (Ambra). Restless, cannot remain in one position. Fright and its effects.
sections.Head: Fullness, heavy, pulsating, hot, bursting headache. Burning in brain. Vertigo worse on rising. Sensation as if hair were pulled. Brain feels as if moving in waves.
sections.Eyes: Red, inflamed, feeling as if sand under lids. Eyes feel dry and hot. Conjunctivitis. Profuse watering after exposure to dry cold winds.
sections.Ears: Very sensitive to sounds. Music is unbearable. Earache. External ear hot, red, painful, swollen.
sections.Nose: Smell acutely sensitive. Pain at root of nose. Coryza; dry, or with watery discharge. Nosebleed.
sections.Face: Red, hot, flushed, swollen. Alternating redness and paleness. Neuralgia; worse on left side. Tingling in cheeks and lips.
sections.Mouth: Numb, dry, tingling. Tongue coated white. Bitter taste. Burning and tingling from teeth to stomach.
sections.Stomach: Vomiting with fear, heat, profuse sweat and increased urination. Vomiting, bilious, mucous, bloody. Haematemesis. Burning from mouth to stomach. Coldness of abdomen.
sections.Abdomen: Tense, hot, tympanitic. Sensitive to touch. Abdominal symptoms better after warm soup.
sections.Stool: Hot, bloody, watery. Choleraic discharges. Tenesmus. Nocturnal diarrhoea.
sections.Urine: Retention of urine from cold, from fright. Urging to urinate. Urine hot, scanty, red, bloody.
sections.Male: Crawling and stinging in genitals. Orchitis from suppressed gonorrhoea.
sections.Female: Ovarian neuralgia. Vaginal very sensitive. Vaginismus. Amenorrhoea from fright or cold. Menorrhagia. Labour pains anxious, tossing about.
sections.Respiratory: Constant short, dry cough. Croup, dry, hollow, suffocative. Cough worse at night and after midnight. Pleuritis. Haemoptysis with fear and anxiety. Breathing hard, quickened.
sections.Heart: Tachycardia. Palpitation with anxiety. Pulse full, hard. Pericarditis. Neuralgia of heart.
sections.Extremities: Numbness and tingling. Shooting pains. Weakness and heaviness. Feet insensible. Joints hot, painful, and swollen.
sections.Sleep: Nightmare, wakes up frightened. Sleepless from agitation.
sections.Skin: Hot, dry, burning. Measles. Gooseflesh. Great sensitiveness to touch.
sections.Fever: Cold stage most marked; cold sweat; ice-cold fingertips. High fever; burning, dry skin. No perspiration except on covered parts. Chills and heat alternate.
sections.Dose: Third to thirtieth potency. In aortic diseases, higher potencies.

relationships: Compare: Coff, Cham, Bell. Complementary: Sulph, Calc, Sep. Antidote: Acet-ac, alcohol. Inimical: Acon should not be used before or after Sulph.

general: Arnica produces conditions upon the system quite similar to those resulting from injuries, falls, blows, contusions. Traumatic affections generally; overuse of any organ. The mental disposition corresponds to the physical state; there is a want of sensitiveness, uneasy, as if bruised. It is essentially a traumatic remedy. The great keynote of Arnica is the sore, lame, bruised feeling, as if beaten. Parts lain on feel sore and bruised. Everything on which he lies seems too hard.

sections.Mind: Fears touch or approach of anyone. Answers correctly when spoken to, but relapses. Unconsciousness; when spoken to answers correctly but unconsciousness returns at once. Stupor with involuntary discharge of faeces and urine. Indifference, forgetfulness. Denies that anything is the matter despite serious illness.
sections.Head: Hot head with cold body. Forehead hot; rest of body cool. Bruised, sore feeling. Scalp sore to touch. Painful pressure in forehead after every spell of coughing. Tingling in brain.
sections.Eyes: Diplopia from traumatism. Retinal haemorrhage. Sore, bruised feeling in eyeballs. Bloodshot eyes from mechanical causes or violent coughing.
sections.Mouth: Fetid breath. Toothache after any dental operation.
sections.Stomach: Mouth and throat feel numb. Taste putrid. Desire for vinegar. Great thirst; bitter belching, vomiting of blood or bile. Epigastrium tender. Disgust for meat, milk.
sections.Abdomen: Stitches under false ribs. Distension with purulent, offensive flatus.
sections.Stool: Diarrhoea with fetid, putrid odour; involuntary stool.
sections.Urinary: Retention of urine from over-exertion. Involuntary urination.
sections.Male: Priapism. After sexual excesses, with erections.
sections.Female: Bruised parts after labour. Before and during menses, great fatigue and limbs feel beaten and sore. Nipples very sore.
sections.Respiratory: Coughs with spitting of blood from least exertion. Whooping cough: child cries before coughing. Hoarse and rawness in larynx.
sections.Heart: Angina pectoris; pain radiates to arm and shoulder. Pulse feeble and irregular. Cardiac dropsy. Fatty degeneration.
sections.Extremities: Gout with great fear of being touched or struck by persons coming near him. Rheumatism of muscular tissue. Mechanical injuries from sprains, bruises. Neuritis, tearing, burning pain, worse at night.
sections.Skin: Black and blue spots; petechiae. Rash all over the body. Boils, crops of small boils, very painful.
sections.Sleep: Restless sleep, feels as if the bed is too hard. Dreams of death, injury, mutilations.
sections.Dose: Sixth to two-hundredth potency. For old traumatic cases, higher potencies.

relationships: Compare: Acon, Bapt, Bellis-p, Ham, Rhus-t, Hyper. Antidote: Camph, Ipec, Ign. Complementary: Acon, Hyper, Rhus-t.

general: A profoundly acting remedy on every organ and tissue. Its clear-cut characteristic symptoms and correspondence to many severe types of disease make its homeopathic use most prescribable. Arsenicum affects every organ and tissue. It is especially suited to nervous, restless, anxious dispositions. Great prostration with burning internally. Periodicity and destructiveness are keynote features. Complaints return annually. Burning pains, relieved by heat. Restlessness.

sections.Mind: Great anguish and restlessness. Changes place continually. Fear of death; of being left alone. Miserly, malicious, selfish. Suicidal mania with impulses. Thinks it useless to take medicine. Hypochondriac. After midnight, worse.
sections.Head: Headaches with burning pains, periodic. Scalp very sensitive; cannot brush hair. Head constantly in motion. Vertigo on descending stairs.
sections.Eyes: Burning in eyes with acrid lachrymation. Ulceration of cornea. Oedema around eyes. Spasmodic closure of lids. Iritis.
sections.Nose: Thin, watery, excoriating discharge. Sneezing without relief. Nose feels stopped up. Hay fever with burning in nose and throat.
sections.Face: Pale, yellowish, earthy, sunken, collapsed. Cold sweat. Expression of agony.
sections.Mouth: Dry, bitter, cannot drink enough. Thirst, drinks often but little at a time. Tongue dry, clean, and red. Ulcers on tongue.
sections.Stomach: Cannot bear sight or smell of food. Great thirst; drinks little and often. Heartburn, gulping up of acid and bitter substances. Nausea, retching, and vomiting after eating or drinking. Burning pain. Better from warm drinks.
sections.Abdomen: Acute pain; liver and spleen enlarged and painful. Abdomen swollen and painful. Gastric and enteric fevers.
sections.Stool: Small, offensive, dark, watery stools. Dysentery dark, bloody, with putrid smell. Burning and tenesmus.
sections.Urinary: Scanty, burning, involuntary urination. Albuminuria. Bright's disease.
sections.Female: Menses too profuse and too soon. Leucorrhoea acrid, burning, worse after menses.
sections.Respiratory: Unable to lie down for fear of suffocation. Burning in chest. Expectoration scanty, frothy. Asthma worse midnight. Cough dry, as from sulphur fumes.
sections.Heart: Palpitation with great anxiety and restlessness. Pulse rapid, small, irregular.
sections.Extremities: Trembling, twitching. Weakness in lower limbs. Burning pains in hands and feet.
sections.Skin: Dry, rough, scaly. Cold, blue, livid. Ulcers with offensive discharge. Carbuncles, anthrax.
sections.Fever: High fever with burning. Hectic fever. Chilly; cannot get warm. Septicaemia.
sections.Sleep: Restless; cannot sleep after 2 a.m. from anxiety. Vivid, disagreeable dreams.
sections.Dose: Second to thirtieth potency. The very high potencies often yield brilliant results.

relationships: Complementary: Thuj, Carbo-v, Phos, All-s. Antidote: Camph, Ip, Merc, Nux-v, China. Compare: Phos, China, Verat.

general: The mental symptoms chiefly are brought to mind by this drug — hopelessness, despondency, and disgust of life. It also affects deeply the liver, heart, and vascular system. Bone destruction and periostitis. Syphilitic affections. It has a special affinity for glands — lymph nodes, thyroid, parotid. Puffy, congested, bluish-red face.

sections.Mind: Profound despondency, with increased impressionability, peevishness, loathing of life, tendency to suicide. Feeling of self-condemnation and utter worthlessness. Profound melancholy. Talks of committing suicide. Hopeless; despondent. Oversensitive; feels criticised. Religious melancholia.
sections.Head: Violent pain in bones of skull. Periostitis. Exostoses painful to touch. Tearing pains. Hyperaemia of brain.
sections.Eyes: Photophobia. Half sight — upper half of vision lost. Syphilitic iritis. Scleritis.
sections.Ears: Parotid glands swollen. Detonation in ears like a report. Sensitive to noise.
sections.Nose: Ulceration and caries of nasal bones. Fetid discharge. Boring pain at root of nose. Very sensitive to smell.
sections.Face: Red, bluish-red, puffy. Jaundice. Tearing pains in bones of face.
sections.Mouth: Foul breath. Spongy gums. Ulceration in mouth. Taste bitter. Mercurial salivation with tearing bone pains.
sections.Heart: Violent palpitation; pulse rapid, feeble, irregular. High blood pressure. Fatty degeneration. Aortitis. Valvular lesions. Hypertrophy. Pericarditis with effusion.
sections.Abdomen: Liver greatly enlarged. Ascites. Jaundice. Spleen affections. Hepatitis.
sections.Urinary: Urine of high specific gravity. Nephritis. Albuminuria.
sections.Male: Loss of sexual desire. Atrophy of testes. Gonorrhoea. Orchitis.
sections.Female: Uterine complaints, prolapse of uterus. Painful induration of uterus. Amenorrhoea.
sections.Extremities: Nightly bone pains in arms and legs. Tearing pains in joints. Anchylosis of shoulder joint. Rheumatism.
sections.Dose: Sixth to thirtieth potency and higher. Very high potencies for mental symptoms.

relationships: Compare: Aur-m, Aur-m-n, Puls, Ign, Plat, Merc. Complementary: Ars, Cup, Dig.
